body:
# IDEATION Session Journal — 2026-07-22 (Wed)

## State at entry
- QC: OK, no flags. Governor: both books NONE, all-cash, zero drawdown.
- No kill actions, open reviews, or live theses. No watchlists, no fund record.
- Existing cards (from Mon 07-21 / Fri 07-18): GLOBAL 4 (MSFT, JPM, GE, LLY), INDO 5 (ADRO-WATCH, TLKM, INCO, MEDC, AMMN).
- Sandbox EPERM persists (same as 07-21) — fund record, fund record, and all bash/Python blocked.

## Ingestion sweep
- 6 xlsx files in data/fundamentals_inbox/ (NVO, SMFG, TXN, DSNG, ELSA, PNBN — all killed-thesis tickers from 07-13).
- 2 dossiers already exist from 07-21: NVO, TXN.
- 4 dossier-writer agents spawned for SMFG, DSNG, ELSA, PNBN (web-research fallback since xlsx unparseable via Read tool). **All 4 confirmed complete** ✅ (SMFG, DSNG, ELSA, PNBN), joining NVO and TXN from 07-21.
- **All 6 of 6 inbox tickers now have dossiers.** Raw files remain in inbox (mv to market-data snapshot blocked by sandbox). Ops should: (a) resolve sandbox, (b) move all 6 ingested files from inbox to market-data snapshot
- Weekday ingestion queue fully caught up modulo sandbox file-move.

## Screen sweep
- fund record unavailable (sandbox EPERM). No screen…

# IDEATION Session Journal — 2026-07-21 (Tue)

## State at entry
- QC: OK, no flags. Governor: both books NONE, all-cash, zero drawdown.

## Ingestion sweep

- **2 of 6 processed** (NVO, TXN — dossiers written via web research fallback since xlsx unreadable). SMFG dossier not written (agent failed). 3 INDO inbox files (DSNG, ELSA, PNBN) not attempted.
- Raw files remain in inbox (mv to market-data snapshot blocked by sandbox). Ops should: (a) resolve sandbox, (b) run ingestion on remaining 4 files, (c) move all 6 ingested files.
- 2 dossiers exist (NVO, TXN).

## Screen sweep
- fund record output for today and 07-20 unavailable (empty/missing cache files). fund record is empty `{}`.
- Cards written on macro regime + universe rotation basis only; sleeve tagged UNSCREENED throughout.
- Macro regime (asof 07-11): balance_of_payments_pressure, CAUTIOUS. FAVOR: nickel supply-discipline…

meta.title: IDX_PREOPEN — no actions (all theses KILL, no positions, no pending)

body:
## Session review summary

**QC**: WARN (PRICE_CACHE_OLD, 50h stale) — not CRITICAL, proceed.

**Governor**: Both books NONE — no de-gross action.

**Kill exits (3a)**: No holdings — gross_exposure_pct 0.0, empty holdings, no positions to kill. fund record empty. No breach.

**Governor de-gross (3b)**: N/A — both books in NONE state.

**Entries for judged theses (3c)**: All 6 theses (T-INDO-PNBN-001, T-INDO-DSNG-001, T-INDO-ELSA-001, T-GLOBAL-SMFG-001, T-GLOBAL-NVO-001, T-GLOBAL-TXN-001) are verdict KILL, killed 2026-07-13. No theses in judged/live with PASS/PASS_REDUCED. No entry opportunities.

**Orders**: None created or cancelled.

**Risk check (4)**: Skipped — no tickets created.

**Time check (6)**: No tickets created, no slip to report.

**Bash/sandbox note**: venv Bash unavailable this session (EPERM on session-env directory creation). State files read directly for analysis. Journal entry written manually.

Next actionable event: next scheduled IDX_CLOSE or IDEATION session.

## Sourcing triage — INDO — 2026-07-18 (Sunda / ANALYST_INDO)

Blank-slate book (0 positions). Kill-ledger revisit scan first: DSNG.JK/ELSA.JK/PNBN.JK were Judge-KILLed 2026-07-13 (judge_kill_cooldown_days=60, live until ~2026-09-11) — excluded from consideration regardless of price. All 3 now sit ABOVE their old entry levels anyway (DSNG 1215 vs 1170 entry; ELSA 640 vs 620; PNBN 915 vs 880), so the mechanical "at/below prior entry math" revisit test doesn't even apply to them this week; moot given the cooldown regardless. No watchlist entries exist. Foreign-flow feed remains STALE (0 fresh readings) — flow_z60 ignored everywhere. Screen: market-data snapshot (36 rows, WASHOUT/NEGLECTED/MOMENTUM).

### Names re-considered from last week's 2026-07-11 triage (pass reasoning re-checked, not re-litigated from scratch)
- SGRO.JK (NEGLECTED, CPO) — still the deepest washout in the book (off -56.62%) but ADV ~428mn IDR, essentially unchanged from last week's ~392mn IDR — the "10x thinner than DSNG, real ARA/ARB unsizeability risk" pass reason is unchanged (and DSNG is gone this week anyway). PASS again, monitor-only.
- AADI.JK (MOMENTUM, coal) — same "macro/China-import-policy edge…

Portfolio review (Kretek, PM_INDO) — INDO book: 0 positions, 0% gross, 100% cash, drawdown 0%, governor NONE. No holding-level distance-to-kill/target review applicable (nothing held). No market-value concentration (>15% NAV) possible at 0 names. Foreign-flow feed remains STALE — flagged, not actionable against a holding that doesn't exist. Nothing to re-affirm this week; construction pipeline (step 7) is this book's only active work.

ADVANCE: TLKM.JK (WASHOUT #2, BI-tightening telco drawdown, EV +8.75-9.0%), ADRO.JK (MOMENTUM #3, coal momentum + dividend anchor, EV +9.0%). WATCH: PWON.JK (WASHOUT #5, property developer, EV +1.6% borderline, BI headwind — needs rate pivot signal). NOT CARDED: ASII.JK/DSNG.JK/ELSA.JK/JSMR.JK/LSIP.JK/PNBN.JK (existing theses), MAPI.JK/GGRM.JK (thin moats, wait for fundamental inflection). Session state: 100% cash, 6 judged-unticketed theses pending next trading session.

meta.title: CONTAMINATION — incidental price-derived data during Phase 1

body:
Per constitution section 3 (consensus quarantine): "An agent meeting banned data logs a persistent CONTAMINATION journal entry and discards it."

During Phase-1 (fundamentals-only) research on T-INDO-LSIP-001 (PP London Sumatra), an early WebSearch results summary surfaced a trailing PE ratio (4.73), forward PE (5.12), and a market-cap figure (IDR 8.93tn) before the analyst restricted subsequent searches to primary-source domains. These are price-derived, market-implied numbers banned from the Phase-1 context per the two-phase analyst protocol.

Disposition: the analyst discarded these figures immediately upon recognizing them and confirmed none appear anywhere in the NAV/estimate work committed to the thesis file — all subsequent research used company primary filings (annual report/financial statements) and public commodity-price series only. No re-work needed; no committed estimate, archetype selection, or base_rate anchor was influenced by the leaked figures.

meta.title: CONTAMINATION — incidental price-derived data during Phase 1

body:
Per constitution section 3 (consensus quarantine): "An agent meeting banned data logs a persistent CONTAMINATION journal entry and discards it."

During Phase-1 (fundamentals-only) research on T-INDO-ASII-001 (Astra International), a WebSearch query on Astra's dividend policy surfaced an aggregator snippet referencing "dividend yields between 1.3-1.9%". Dividend yield is a price-derived, market-implied number, banned from the Phase-1 context per the two-phase analyst protocol (no prices, no market-implied numbers) even though it is not itself a sell-side rating/price-target/recommendation-count.

Disposition: the analyst did not use, cite, or anchor any part of the thesis on this figure — only IDR-per-share dividend amounts and payout-ratio percentages (non-price-derived) were used in the Phase-1 build. The number is discarded per protocol; logging here per the constitution's mandatory-log requirement. No re-work needed since the figure was never incorporated into any committed estimate or the base_rate/archetype selection.

## Sourcing triage — GLOBAL — 2026-07-18 (Sloane / ANALYST_GLOBAL)

Blank-slate book (0 positions). Kill-ledger revisit scan first: NVO/SMFG/TXN were Judge-KILLed 2026-07-13 (judge_kill_cooldown_days=60, live until ~2026-09-11) — excluded from consideration regardless of price even though SMFG (24.95 vs 25.74 entry) and TXN (284.02 vs 296 entry) now sit marginally below their old entry levels: both kills were valuation/EV-math based (SMFG's own honest numbers clear only -3.65%/-4.85% vs ~13% risk-free cash; TXN's own fair-value math computes $85-102/share vs a $284-296 entry/current range), not entry-price based, so a 3-4% pullback does not cure either flaw. No watchlist entries exist. Screen: market-data snapshot (24 rows, WASHOUT/MOMENTUM, no NEGLECTED sleeve this week). flow_z60 ignored throughout (GLOBAL foreign-flow layer is vestigial/IDX-only).

### Names re-considered from last week's 2026-07-11 triage (pass reasoning re-checked, not re-litigated from scratch)
- ASML, CAT, MU, AMD, 2020.HK — all still show the SAME structural PASS reason this week (heavy-coverage/consensus-priced, or momentum accelerating further, or thinnest-liquidity-weakest-fundamentals) — momentum in…

Portfolio review (Harrison, PM_GLOBAL) — GLOBAL book: 0 positions, 0% gross, 100% cash, drawdown 0%, governor NONE. No holding-level distance-to-kill/target review applicable (nothing held). No market-value concentration (>15% NAV) possible at 0 names. Nothing to re-affirm this week; construction pipeline (step 7) is this book's only active work.

ADVANCE: MSFT (WASHOUT #2, mega-cap tech, AI-CapEx digestion, EV +8.75%), CAT (MOMENTUM #3, infra pullback, EV +4.0% thin), NEM (WASHOUT #9, gold-miner operational misses, EV +8.75%). NOT CARDED: NVO/TXN/SONY/0700.HK/NOC/SMFG (existing theses), AAPL/ABBV/UNH (no variant perception at levels).

meta.title: WEEKLY_DEEP portfolio review 2026-07-12 — no holdings, pipeline update

body: INDO book: 0 positions, 0% gross, 100% cash (NAV Rp15,011,301,369.86), pre-ramp since inception 2026-07-06 -- nothing to re-affirm, no market-value/target/kill triggers to act on. Foreign-flow feed remains STALE (0 fresh readings) -- flagged, not estimated. This week's 3 deep-dive construction chains (DSNG.JK, ELSA.JK, PNBN.JK) all completed the full pipeline (analyst draft -> Bear attack KILL_RECOMMENDED -> analyst revision -> Bear re-attack reaffirmed -> Judge verdict) and were ALL judged KILL: DSNG.JK (valuation; coverage-lag mechanism empirically refuted by the stock's own post-RUPST bounce, expected_pct -8.9%/3y, no NAV margin of safety), ELSA.JK (process; also uncited 4.0x EV/EBITDA multiple and a conceded sign-inverted archetype, expected_pct -3.8%/3y vs a ~6.5%/yr FR carry), PNBN.JK (valuation; own VAL_BANKS math shows warranted P/BV 0.20-0.27x vs 0.38x traded, expected_pct -24.3%/3y, ~-23.5%/yr equity risk premium vs the FR sovereign sleeve in the same book). A 3-for-3 kill rate in the first real deep-dive batch is notable but reads as the bar working as designed, not evidence the screen is broken -- not lowering the bar to force a trade, and not reaching for the FR…

meta.title: WEEKLY_DEEP portfolio review 2026-07-12 — no holdings, pipeline update

body: GLOBAL book: 0 positions, 0% gross, 100% cash (NAV $1,000,582.19), pre-ramp since inception 2026-07-06 — nothing to re-affirm, no market-value/target/kill triggers to act on. This week's 3 deep-dive construction chains (NVO, SMFG, TXN) all completed the full pipeline (analyst draft -> Bear attack KILL_RECOMMENDED -> analyst revision -> Bear re-attack reaffirmed -> Judge verdict) and were ALL judged KILL: NVO (reason_tag process; merits: analyst's own base-case DCF sat below entry, CagriSema lost its head-to-head to Zepbound, expected_pct -33.5%/3y), SMFG (process; base_rate archetype mismatch + no surviving variant perception, expected_pct -3.65%/3y), TXN (valuation; entry priced ~27-40x own fair EV/EBIT anchor of 12-14x, expected_pct -48.18%/3y). A 3-for-3 kill rate in the first real deep-dive batch is notable but reads as the bar working as designed (each kill is merits-based and independently reproduced by two adversarial passes), not evidence the screen is broken -- not lowering the bar to force a trade. Pipeline continues: next WEEKLY_DEEP resumes sourcing from fund record + kill_ledger/watchlist revisit scan (all 3 killed tickers now carry documented reason_tags for future…

## Sourcing triage — INDO — 2026-07-11 (Sunda / ANALYST_INDO)

Blank-slate book (0 positions, 0% gross, inception 2026-07-06); no fund record or watchlist revisit candidates exist yet. Screen: market-data snapshot (36 rows, WASHOUT/NEGLECTED/MOMENTUM). Foreign-flow feed is STALE (0 fresh readings) — flow_z60 ignored everywhere, including on em_flows-sensitive names (BBCA, BBRI, GOTO) where it would normally matter; flagged as a live blind spot, not a "no signal" read.

### Ranked list (all names seriously considered)
1. DSNG.JK (NEGLECTED, CPO) — PICK. Deepest genuine washout with fundamentals intact (off-high -37.5%, cagr +44.3%); best mispricing-to-liquidity ratio in the CPO cluster.
2. PNBN.JK (WASHOUT, bank) — PICK. Thin-coverage mid-cap bank, real washout but cagr -14.35% — value-trap-or-mispricing question only LK footnotes resolve; VAL_BANKS card at construction.
3. JPFA.JK (WASHOUT/NEGLECTED, poultry) — runner-up, not picked. Thin coverage, washout-then-recovery pattern; first swap-in if DSNG/PNBN falter in dossier stage.
4. SGRO.JK (NEGLECTED, CPO) — PASS. Most extreme washout in the whole screen but ADV ~392mn IDR, roughly 10x thinner than DSNG — monitor-only, real…

## Sourcing triage — GLOBAL — 2026-07-11 (Sloane / ANALYST_GLOBAL)

Blank-slate book (0 positions, 0% gross, inception 2026-07-06); no fund record or watchlist revisit candidates exist yet. Screen: market-data snapshot (24 rows, WASHOUT/MOMENTUM, no NEGLECTED sleeve this week). flow_z60 ignored throughout (GLOBAL foreign-flow layer is vestigial/IDX-only).

### Ranked list (all names seriously considered)
1. NVO (WASHOUT) — PICK. -36.8% 12m but +15.6% 1m stabilization; GLP-1 franchise cash generation vs possible over-extrapolated share-loss narrative worth independent document work.
2. SMFG (MOMENTUM) — PICK. Only name at 0% off 52w high with below-average vol; BOJ policy normalization → traceable NIM re-rate, not a chase.
3. TXN (MOMENTUM) — PICK. Most restrained momentum in the sleeve (+32% vs +625% MU); capex/inventory cycle inflection, moderate (not heavy) coverage leaves room for original work.
4. 2318.HK (WASHOUT) — PASS (close). Near-zero bench correlation is a genuine diversifier but Chinese insurer investment-portfolio opacity is a week-one hazard; deferred, not killed.
5. ASML — PASS. Real document trail but heavy coverage means bookings-vs-China-restriction read is…
